en.extract:
The southern yellow white-eye (Zosterops anderssoni) is a bird species in the family Zosteropidae. It is found in parts of southern Africa. It was formerly considered conspecific with the African yellow white-eye.
The southern yellow white-eye was formerly treated as a subspecies of the African yellow white-eye, (renamed the northern yellow white-eye), (Zosterops senegalensis) but it is now considered as a separate species based on the phylogenetic relationships determined in a molecular study in 2013.
Three subspecies are recognised:

Z. a. anderssoni Shelley, 1892 – east and south Angola and north Namibia to southwest Tanzania, west Mozambique and north South Africa
Z. a. tongensis Roberts, 1931 – southeast Zimbabwe, south Mozambique and northeast South Africa
Z. a. stierlingi Reichenow, 1899 – east and south Tanzania, east Zambia, Malawi and north Mozambique
Its diet consists of insects, fruits, seeds, and grains.

The '''southern yellow white-eye''' ('''''Zosterops anderssoni''''') is a bird [[species]] in the family [[Zosteropidae]]. It is found in parts of southern Africa. It was formerly considered [[conspecific]] with the African yellow white-eye.

The southern yellow white-eye was formerly treated as a [[subspecies]] of the African yellow white-eye, (renamed the [[northern yellow white-eye]]), (''Zosterops senegalensis'') but it is now considered as a separate species based on the [[phylogenetic]] relationships determined in a molecular study in 2013.<ref>{{ cite thesis | last=Cox | first=S.C. | year=2013 | title=Molecular Systematics and Diversification of African Zosteropidae (Aves: Passeriformes) | type=PhD | institution=University College London | url=http://discovery.ucl.ac.uk/1397762/ }}</ref><ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Pearson | first1=D.J. | last2=Turner | first2=D.A. | year=2017 | title=A taxonomic review of the genus ''Zosterops'' in East Africa, with a revised list of species occurring in Kenya, Uganda and Tanzania | journal=Scopus  | volume=37 | pages=1–13 | url=https://www.ajol.info/index.php/scopus/article/viewFile/150534/140112 }}</ref><ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | year=2019 | title=Sylviid babblers, parrotbills, white-eyes | work=World Bird List Version 9.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/sylvias/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=24 January 2019 }}</ref>

Three subspecies are recognised:<ref name=ioc/>
* ''Z. a. anderssoni'' [[George Ernest Shelley|Shelley]], 1892 – east and south Angola and north Namibia to southwest Tanzania, west Mozambique and north South Africa
* ''Z. a. tongensis'' [[Austin Roberts (zoologist)|Roberts]], 1931 – southeast Zimbabwe, south Mozambique and northeast South Africa
* ''Z. a. stierlingi'' [[Anton Reichenow|Reichenow]], 1899 – east and south Tanzania, east Zambia, Malawi and north Mozambique
Its diet consists of insects, fruits, seeds, and grains.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Ojija |first1=Fredrick |last2=Manyanza |first2=Ndaki Marco |date=2022-03-07 |title=Community Structure, Conservation Status, and Functional Groups of Bird Species in Mbeya Range Forests, Tanzania |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.29169/1927-5129.2022.18.05 |journal=Journal of Basic & Applied Sciences |volume=18 |pages=33–46 |doi=10.29169/1927-5129.2022.18.05 |issn=1927-5129}}</ref>
